#0   Fertiliser subsidy to double; fisc in control: Govt sources

page: 1 section: Economy lang: en crop: text text: ocr confidence: 0.92 verified

Sub-headline: Officials see no need for additional borrowing

Byline: Prasanta Sahu, New Delhi

BODY TEXT  (ocr — grounded record)
PRASANTA SAHU New Delhi, June 9 DESPITE MOUNTING PRES- SURE from surging global commodity prices that could nearly double the Centre’s fer- tiliser subsidy bill to ¥3.4 lakh crore and necessitate support of ¥1.23 lakh crore for oil mar- keting companies, the govern- ment remains firmly in con- trol of the fiscal situation and sees little risk to India’s growth momentum, senior officials said. Even as geopolitical ten- sions in West Asia and global trade uncertainties cloud the external environment, officials said the economy continues to be supported by resilient domestic demand and a strong underlying growth trajectory. According to sources, the Union Budget for FY27 had already built in a degree of global uncertainty — now a recurring feature of the eco- nomic landscape — limiting FORTIFYING FINANCES the need for additional bor- rowing or supplementary demands for grants during the upcoming Monsoon Session of Parliament. The fiscal deficit target of 4.3% of the GDP, they stressed, remains unchanged. The government is simul- taneously stepping up efforts to mobilise non-tax revenues Disinvestment and asset monet isation push: BL sales in state-run companies and expects the proceeds to exceed Budget estimate of ¥80,000 cr in FY27 through disinvestment and asset monet isation to offset part of the fiscal strain from higher subsidy commitments. A senior official said the fer- tiliser department has pro- jected subsidy requirements of up to 3.4 lakh crore this fiscal year, compared with the bud- geted 1.71 lakh crore, as Foreign capital inflows: Tax exemptions
Vision summary (AI — unverified)
Despite mounting pressure from surging global commodity prices that nearly double the Centre's fertiliser subsidy bill to ₹3.4 lakh crore and necessitate support for marketing and distribution, the government remains firmly in control of the fiscal situation and growth momentum, senior officials said. Even as geopolitical tensions in West Asia and global trade uncertainties cloud the external environment, officials expect to be supported by resilient domestic demand and a strong underlying growth trajectory. A senior official said the Union Budget for FY27 had already built in a degree of fiscal slippage. Sources, however, said the fertiliser subsidy requirements of up to ₹3.4 lakh crore - now a recurring feature of the economic landscape - limiting the government's ability to cut expenditures. The government has been steadily stepping up efforts to mobilise non-tax revenues through disinvestment and asset monetisation to offset a part of the fiscal strain from higher subsidy commitments. A senior official said the fertiliser disbursal requirements of up to ₹3.4 lakh crore, compared with the budgeted ₹2.71 lakh crore, as the subsidy bill has crossed ₹1.71 lakh crore, as against ₹80,000 crore in FY26.

#1   Zepto emerges as challenger to Blinkit

page: 1 section: Economy lang: en crop: text text: ocr confidence: 0.88 verified

Byline: Ayanti Bera, Bengaluru, June 9

BODY TEXT  (ocr — grounded record)
AYANTI BERA Bengaluru, June 9 ZEPTO'S MARKET DEBUT would make it the third major quick-commerce player with publicly disclosed financials, increasing transparency in a sector projected to grow five to seven times by 2030. The lat- est numbers suggest Blinkit retains a sizeable lead, but Zepto has narrowed the battle to a two-horse race on growth and scale, setting up a more intense contest for investors and consumers alike. A comparison of operating metrics disclosed in Zepto’s updated draft red herring prospectus and publicly avail- able financial data from Eter- nal and Swiggy shows Blinkit PECKING ORDER i Blinkit Jl Zepto J Instamart Orders in FY26 (mn) ZEPTO DOUBLES REVENUE AHEAD OF IPO P4 remains the largest player across most key measures. However, Zepto has estab- lished a clear lead over Average HEI RR Bi daily orders 25 (mn) Dark stores 2243 1139 1143 A A FY26 revenue (zcrore) RE 37,779 Bl 22.624 Bl 3859 Swiggy-owned Instamart on order volumes, order value and network productivity. Blinkit processed net orders worth ¥48,567 crore inFY26,nearly double Zepto’s net order value of 324,816 crore. Instamart reported 1.8 11 gross order value of ¥28,496 crore during the year, which translates into a lower net order value on a comparable basis. Blinkit also processed 916.6 million orders in the last financial year, compared with 640 million orders for Zepto and 412 million orders for Instamart. On a daily basis, Blinkit handled around 2.5 million orders, while Zepto processed 1.8 million and Instamart about 1.1 million. Blinkit’s leadership is also reflected in its infrastructure footprint. on
Vision summary (AI — unverified)
Zepto's market debut would make it the third major quick-commerce player with publicly disclosed financials, increasing pressure in a sector projected to grow five to seven times by 2030. The last few years have seen Blinkit, Zepto, and Swiggy's Blinkit retain a sizeable lead, but Zepto has narrowed the battle for a two-horse race on growth and scale, setting up a intense contest for investors and consumers alike. Zepto's operational metrics disclosed in its updated draft red herring prospectus and publicly available data suggest Blinkit remains the largest player. However, Zepto has established a clear lead over Swiggy-owned Instamart on order volumes, order value and network density. Blinkit processed net orders worth ₹48,567 crore in FY26, nearly double Zepto's orders in FY25. Instamart reported gross order value of ₹28,496 crore during the year, which translates into a market share of 37.6%. On a daily basis, Blinkit handled around 2.5 million orders, while Zepto processed about 1.1 million. Instamart's order value is also reflected in its infrastructure footprint.

#4   AI agents may equal TCS' human workforce in 3 yrs

page: 1 section: Business lang: en crop: text text: ocr confidence: 0.91 verified

Byline: N Chandrasekaran, Chairman, TCS

BODY TEXT  (ocr — grounded record)
Hiring set to slow but no headcount reduction: Chandra URVI MALVANIA Mumbai, June 9 ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COULD become as large a workforce as humans at Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) within the next three years, Chairman N Chandrasekaran said on Tuesday, signalling a shift that could reduce the industry’s hiring intensity as the company scales up its Al business, which has already reached an annualised revenue run-rate of $2.4 billion. “I'predictthat over the next three years, TCS will have as many Al agents as human employees,” Chandrasekaran said at the company’s annual general meeting, held virtually. “Will it lead to decrease in hir- ing? Absolutely. The company will not be hiring the kind of numbers it used to hire.” Putting a number to the company’s Al ambitions, Chandrasekaran said that if TCS has around half a million employees, “the day is not far when the company has half a million AI agents”. He said AUTO ZONE N CHANDRASEKARAN, CHAIRMAN, TCS If the company has half a million employees, the day is not far when the company will have half a million AI agents The company’s employees and AI agents will work together, and that will be the future By 2028 to 2030, 100% of the revenue will have an Al component parts of the work currently per- formed by employees would increasingly be undertaken by Al systems, changing the industry’s traditional people- led delivery model. However, he ruled out workforce reductions, saying TCS would continue to invest in talent and reskilling. “There is no downsizing of staff. That is not planned at all,” Chan- drasekaran said, adding that the focus would be on enabling humans and Al agents towork together. He also said Alwould create new categories of jobs and opportunities over time. The comments come at a time when investors are debat- ingthelong-term implications of generative Al for the IT ser- vices industry. Chan- drasekaran said that every major technology disruption has historically expanded technology spending and cre- ated new opportunities for technology companies rather than reducing demand. on
Vision summary (AI — unverified)
If the company has half a million employees, the day is not far when the company will have half a million AI agents but not headcount reduction: Chandra Urvi Malvania, Mumbai, June 9 The company's employees and AI agents will work together, and that will be the future. By 2028 to 2030, 100% of the revenue will have an AI component. That's how the company's annual report says it. "We believe that over the next few years, many AI agents as human employees will be employed." Chandrasekaran said that the company's AI ambitions, or "Tech-Stack" as he calls it, are not downscaling of staff. That "Tech-Stack" has been built for long-term growth. TCS has around half a million employees globally. "Will it lead to increase in headcount? Absolutely. The company will not be hiring the kind of people we are hiring today."

#5   Trai mulls network slicing regulations

page: 1 section: Business lang: en crop: text text: ocr confidence: 0.9 verified

Byline: Rishi Raj, New Delhi, June 9

BODY TEXT  (ocr — grounded record)
Telecom regulator monitoring Airtel’s Priority Postpaid RISHI RAJ New Delhi, June 9 THE TELECOM REGULA- TORY Authority of India (Trai) is adopting a two-pronged approach towards Bharti Air- tel’s recently launched Prior- ity Postpaid service, monitor- ing whether the offering affects service quality for pre- paid subscribers while exam- ining the need for a broader regulatory framework for net- work slicing-based consumer services, according to people aware of the discussions. The move comes after Air- tel launched Priority Postpaid, a service that uses 5G network slicing technology to offer a more stable and consistent network experience to post- paid users during periods of high traffic demand. The launch has triggered a debate within the telecom industry and among policymakers over whether differentiated net- work experiences for different
Vision summary (AI — unverified)
The telecom regulator is adopting a two-pronged approach towards Bharti Airtel's recently launched Priority Postpaid service, monitoring whether the offering affects service quality for prepaid users while examining if there's a need for a better network experience to post-paid users during periods of high traffic demand. The move comes after Airtel launched Priority Postpaid, a service that uses 5G network slicing technology to offer a more stable and consistent network experience to post-paid users. The regulator has sought views on whether the introduction of network slicing technology results in any deterioration in service quality for prepaid users.

#8   New Mehti missive deepens Tata row

page: 1 section: Business lang: en crop: none text: vision

Byline: Urvi Malvania, Mumbai, June 9

BODY TEXT  (vision — grounded record)
Former Tata Trusts' trustee Mehti Mistry has significantly escalated his challenge to the current leadership structure of the Tata Trusts. In filings before the Mumbai High Court, Mistry has sought a raft of governance reforms. He has raised questions on whether remuneration received from group companies by trustees is consistent with the fiduciary obligations expected of those overseeing charitable institutions.

#9   Siri jumps on AI bandwagon in more 'personal' avatar

page: 1 section: Opinion lang: en crop: text text: ocr confidence: 0.93 verified

Byline: Craig Federighi, Cupertino (US), June 9

BODY TEXT  (ocr — grounded record)
ANUJ BHATIA that you use in the moment,” awareness. For instance, it can
Vision summary (AI — unverified)
We use none of the models that Google deploys to its customers. Your requests are completely private to you. They're never stored. They're never accessible to anyone. Unlike previous versions, the new Siri can not only understand your personal context but also perform actions on your behalf across apps, while remaining aware of what's on your screen through Apple's Visual Intelligence. Siri will also be able to search the web for answers, much like ChatGPT and Claude. Siri is also gaining on-screen awareness. For instance, it can understand actions on a user's behalf. That was always the original vision for Siri, which was launched more than a decade ago. It took many years for Apple to overcome the biggest hurdle: that users weren't ready to call out information on their screen. The company first announced this next-generation Siri in June 2024 along with the side launch of Apple Intelligence. But the AI-powered version faced repeated delays.

#10   Epic, Sekura emerge as highest bidders for NHAI ToT assets

page: 3 section: Economy lang: en crop: text text: ocr confidence: 0.92 verified

Sub-headline: MONETISATION PUSH

Byline: Mukesh Jagota, New Delhi, June 9

BODY TEXT  (ocr — grounded record)
MUKESH JAGOTA New Delhi, June 9 SEKURA ROADS AND Epic Infrastructure, two platforms under Edelweiss Alternatives, have emerged as the highest bidders for two highway stretches being monetised through Toll Operate Transfer (ToT), by putting in a bid of 2,259 crore. It is the first instance of highway asset monet isation in this financial year by the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI). The stretches on offer are the Trichy-Thanjavur section and the Madurai-Tuticorin on National Highway 38, totalling 184.5 km in Tamil Nadu. These stretches are part of the ToT Bundle 19, which was first bid outin June 2024 and saw many extensions of the bid submission deadline. The financial bids were finally opened last week. Before finalising the even- tual concessionaire for the highways a few more levels of approval are required. Apart from Sekura-Epic there were three other bid- ders for the assets. In ToT mode of monetisa- tion, the concessionaire gets the right to collect toll for 20 years on the won stretches in return for an upfront pay- ment. Other ToT Bundles for which bids are currently open are Bundles 20, 21 and 22. For this financial year, the
Vision summary (AI — unverified)
Sekura Roads and Epic Infrastructure Alternatives, under Edelweiss Alternatives, have emerged as the highest bidders for two highway stretches being monetised through Toll Operate Transfer (ToT), by putting in a bid of ₹2,259 crore. Apart from Sekura-Epic there were 3 other bidders for assets and the Madurai-Puducherry stretch on National Highway 38, totalling 184.5 km in Tamil Nadu. The stretches on offer are Trichy-Thanjavur section of NH 38 totalling 184.5 km and the Madurai-Puducherry section on National Highway 38 totalling 184.5 km in Tamil Nadu. These stretches are part of the ToF Bundle 3, which was bid out in June 2024 and saw many extensions of the bidding deadline. The financial bids were finally opened last week. The concessionaire gets the right to toll collection for 20 years on the stretches being monetised. Other ToT Bundles for which bids are currently under evaluation are Bundles 20, 21 and 23.

#11   Only deepfakes to face action, says Vaishnaw

page: 3 section: Economy lang: en crop: text text: ocr confidence: 0.91 verified

Byline: Press Trust of India, New Delhi, June 9

BODY TEXT  (ocr — grounded record)
entity Cockroach Janata Party PRESS TRUST OF INDIA aredoing. In terms of per capita
Vision summary (AI — unverified)
The government will take action only against deep fake content with no clampdown on genuine content creation, Union Minister for Electronics and IT Ashwini Vaishnaw said on Tuesday, debunking allegations of censorship. Recently, there have been several claims on removal of online videos on social media platforms. The minister debunked claims that genuine protest videos are being removed from social media platforms. 'Absolutely not. Wherever these fake videos are, it's a duty, it has to be taken down. Then it has to be ensured that if any deep fake video is propagating false, actually false news, then the trust has to be strengthened.' The Information Technology law was framed much before the rapid emergence of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said on Tuesday, adding that he believes a new legal framework may be required to deal with the changing landscape of AI. 'The current Information Technology law was framed much before the rapid emergence of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said on Tuesday, adding that he believes a new legal framework may be required to deal with the changing landscape of AI.'

#12   'New AI-alleged law needed'

page: 3 section: Economy lang: en crop: text text: ocr confidence: 0.9 verified

Byline: PTI

BODY TEXT  (ocr — grounded record)
THE CURRENT INFORMA- TION technology law was framed much before the rapid emergence of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Union Min- ister Ashwini Vaishnaw said on Tuesday, adding that he believes a new legal frame-
Vision summary (AI — unverified)
Vaishnaw told PTI that the society is built on trust between institutions and that trust has to be strengthened. 'The point is that there is a need to ensure that if you see whether that is a real or fake, that has to be ensured through various technological methods, and that is what we are working on. If you look at it, it is not even a fraction of the content that is created, which is basically deepfake and which is basically a problem. We have to build it.' The minister said that there is a need to remove fake content, irrespective of who generates it. The amendment creates the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting's (MIB) oversight mechanism.
